title: senecio sarracenicus var. latifolius
intro: Senecio sarracenicus var. latifolius, also known as the Broad-leaved Jerusalem Cross or Fern-leaved Ragwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its alkaloid content. Its health benefits include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to support overall well-being.
